zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret de Cralle於大約1356年出生在英國英格蘭素可時，她的父親Sir Robert de Cralle24歲，母親Lady Margaret de Peplesham22歲。她於大約1380年與Richard Cheyne在英國英格蘭素可瓦布萊頓結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和5個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret de Cralle于大约1356年出生在英国英格兰素可时，她的父亲Sir Robert de Cralle24岁，母亲Lady Margaret de Peplesham22岁。她于大约1380年与Richard Cheyne在英国英格兰素可瓦布萊頓结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和5个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Cralle
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Possibly also an Americanized form of German Kralle (see Krall ).

variants.name: Crawley
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the many places called Crawley, for example in Buckinghamshire, Essex, Hampshire, Sussex, and Oxfordshire, named with Old English crāwe ‘crow’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’. Compare Crowley .

Irish (Louth): in Ireland, this is generally the English name in 1 above, but it may also have been adopted for McCrawley, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Raghallaigh ‘son of Raghallach’, also Anglicized as Magreely.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret de Cralle was born about 1356, in Sussex, England, United Kingdom, her father, Sir Robert de Cralle, was 24 and her mother, Lady Margaret de Peplesham, was 22. She married Richard Cheyne about 1380, in Warbleton, Sussex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 5 daughters.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret de Cralle est née environ 1356, à Sussex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Robert de Cralle, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Lady Margaret de Peplesham, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé Richard Cheyne environ 1380, à Warbleton, Sussex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 5 filles.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret de Cralle um 1356 in Sussex, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Robert de Cralle 24 und ihre Mutter Lady Margaret de Peplesham 22. Sie hat um 1380 in Warbleton, Sussex, England, United Kingdom Richard Cheyne geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 5 Töchter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de Cralle è nata il circa 1356 nel Sussex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Sir Robert de Cralle, aveva 24 anni e sua madre, Lady Margaret de Peplesham, aveva 22 anni. Lei sposato Richard Cheyne circa 1380, nel Warbleton, Sussex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 5 figlie.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret de Cralleが約1356年にイギリスイギリススセックスで生まれた時，父Sir Robert de Cralleは24歳，母Lady Margaret de Pepleshamは22歳であった。約1380年にイギリスイギリススセックスワーブレトンでRichard Cheyneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret de Cralle(이)가 대략 1356년 영국 잉글랜드 수세스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Robert de Cralle(은)는 24세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Margaret de Peplesham(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 대략 1380년 영국 잉글랜드 수세스 워블레턴에서 Richard Cheyne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de Cralle nasceu aproximadamente 1356, em Sussex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir Robert de Cralle, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Lady Margaret de Peplesham, tinha 22 anos. Ela casou-se com Richard Cheyne aproximadamente 1380, em Warbleton, Sussex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 5 filhas.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret de Cralle родилась около 1356 г. в местности суссекс, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Sir Robert de Cralle было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Lady Margaret de Peplesham, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard Cheyne около 1380 г. в местности варблетон, суссекс, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 5 дочерей.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret de Cralle nació aproximadamente 1356, en Sussex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir Robert de Cralle, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Lady Margaret de Peplesham, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Richard Cheyne aproximadamente 1380, en Warbleton, Sussex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 5 hijas.
